description: Knowledge base for improving handwritten Greek medical note transcription. Updated with corrections from doctor review.

formatting_rules.medical_terms: PURPLE (#7B2D8E) + UPPERCASE — all disease names, conditions, medical terms
formatting_rules.uncertain_text: RED (#CC0000) + [text?] — words not recognized or not in dictionary

confirmed.ΟΛΛ: Οξεία Λεμφοβλαστική Λευχαιμία (ALL - Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia)
confirmed.Φ: Φυσιολογικό or negative (when used for allergies = No)
confirmed.ΤΚΕ: Ταχύτητα Καθίζησης Ερυθρών (ESR - Erythrocyte Sedimentation Rate)
confirmed.ΠΔΚ: needs confirmation — seen in clinical exam after Οιδήματα

unconfirmed_ask_doctor.Χ/Γ: Seen after surgical procedures — possibly Χειρουργείο/Γενικό? ASK DOCTOR
unconfirmed_ask_doctor.ΑΙ: Seen after Ακουστικό νευρίνωμα — possibly αριστερό (left)? ASK DOCTOR
unconfirmed_ask_doctor.SE: Seen in cardiac exam — possibly systolic ejection? ASK DOCTOR
unconfirmed_ask_doctor.6λφ: Unknown — seen next to Μητέρα in family history. ASK DOCTOR
Seen in instructions — possibly Κλινικός Ομιλιτικός? ASK DOCTOR
unconfirmed_ask_doctor.ΠΔΚ: Seen after Οιδήματα in clinical exam. ASK DOCTOR

note_structure.description: The doctor follows a consistent template for each patient visit
sections_visit: Patient demographics (name, DOB, address, phone, AMKA)
Family history: Self | Father | Mother (3-column). Mother section includes ΕΜΜΗΝΟΠΑΥΣΗ age.
Lab results (empty placeholder if none for this visit)

confirmed.ΕΜΜΗΝΟΠΑΥΣΗ_looks_like_ΕΝΗΜΕΡΩΤΗΣ: In handwriting, ΕΜΜΗΝΟΠΑΥΣΗ can be misread as ΕΝΗΜΕΡΩΤΗΣ — look for it in family history context followed by age
confirmed.Υπεραλδοστερονισμία_was_ΥΠΕΡΛΙΠΙΔΑΙΜΙΑ: Misread ΥΠΕΡΛΙΠΙΔΑΙΜΙΑ as Υπεραλδοστερονισμία — similar letter patterns
confirmed.Προαλβουμινημία_was_ΠΡΟΔΙΑΒΗΤΗΣ: Misread ΠΡΟΔΙΑΒΗΤΗΣ as Προαλβουμινημία — ΠΡΟ prefix correct but rest differs
confirmed.2019_was_2012: Year misread — 2 can look like 9 in this handwriting. Flag uncertain years in red.

suspected.description: Patterns we think we see but need doctor confirmation
items: Doctor writes circled numbers ①②③ for enumerated items
κφ is used as shorthand across all exam systems to mean normal
Lab reference ranges written as (ΦΤ: X-Y) or (ΦΤ < X)
Arrow → used to indicate 'leads to' or 'result is'

hallucination_warnings: Patient004: Θυρεοειδής (ΤΑΚ) and Μασχάλες (κφ) were NOT in the original scan but were generated. Be careful not to add exam items that aren't present.
Always cross-reference every exam line against the scan. Do not assume standard exam items are present.

Patient005: ENTIRE lab results section (CBC + biochemistry, ~30 values) at the bottom of page 1 was MISSED in both v7 and v8. The lab block was written below the family history diagram in a dense horizontal run-on format (values crammed on lines rather than one per row). The model treated the diagram as the 'end' of page 1 and stopped reading. CRITICAL LESSON: Always scroll/read to the absolute bottom of every page. Lab results are often written BELOW diagrams, family trees, or other visual elements in a compressed format.
omission_warnings: PATTERN: Dense horizontal lab blocks at page bottom get missed. When handwritten lab values are written in a compact run-on format (e.g., 'RBC 4,72x10⁶ HGB 13,1 HCT 41,0 MCV 88,1 MCH 27,8...' all on 2-3 lines), the model tends to stop transcribing at the visual landmark above them (e.g., a family tree diagram). After transcribing each page, ALWAYS verify: is there ANY content below the last section I transcribed? Look specifically for dense numeric blocks at the very bottom edge of the page.

Patient005 page 1: Missed CBC (RBC, HGB, HCT, MCV, MCH, MCHC, RDW, WBC, ΤΥΠΟΣ, PLT), inflammatory markers (TKE, CRP), and full biochemistry panel (GLU, Ουρία, Κρεατινίνη, Ουρικό οξύ, SGOT, SGPT, γGT, CHOL, HDL, LDL, Τριγλ) — all from 25/10/2021. These were written in 4 dense lines below the family history diagram.

corrections_log.description: Corrections from doctor review. Each entry maps to a row number in the digitized document.

Patient004_1_v2.note: Phone numbers should always be formatted as XXXX XXX XXX

Referral was from a friend, not a psychiatrist. The original handwriting was φίλη, not Ψυχ. Note: Ψυχ. IS a valid abbreviation for Ψυχίατρος (Psychiatrist) — the error was in reading the handwriting, not the abbreviation meaning.
Father's condition was hyperlipidemia, not hyperaldosteronism.
Father's condition was prediabetes. ΠΡΟ prefix was correct.
Χρ. = Χρόνια (Chronic). Format: abbreviation + full condition name.
In original, this was written to the left of the mother section.

Patient004_1_v2.corrected: Add ΟΙΔΗΜΑΤΑ ΠΔΚ between Νευρολογικά and next item
Patient004_1_v2.note: Edema finding was present in original but missed in transcription.
This should be Αγγειακά (Vascular). Θυρεοειδής was not in the original.
This exam item was hallucinated. Not present in original scan.

common_medical_terms.description: Greek medical terms frequently seen in this doctor's notes. Display in PURPLE + UPPERCASE.

medication_names.description: Medications/pharmaceuticals seen in notes. Display in PURPLE + UPPERCASE. Maintain as running database.

uncertain_rows: Multiple rows on pages 2-4 — VERY LOW confidence for dense handwriting sections
14-15 (history item ② — tongue lesion description very unclear)
Visit 1 hormones/cancer screening (page 2) — nearly all readings uncertain
Visit 2 labs — HDL 29 seems very low, needs verification
Visit 3 CT imaging and ECHO thyroid — largely illegible
Visit 3 DEXA hip T-score — note says osteopenia but value suggests osteoporosis
Visit 4 ALDo/PRA ratio 478 — strongly suggests Conn syndrome (FLAG)
Visit 5 assessment text — nearly all illegible
Visit 8 PTH 209 — extremely elevated, needs verification
Patient001_1_v8.output_format: v8 hybrid — 3-phase transcription + 5 original JPGs appended
Patient001_1_v8.notes: Most complex patient: 5 pages, 8 visits spanning Oct 2014 — Dec 2019. Female, 76yr (at V1), Philologist. Presenting: Right adrenal adenoma (found on MRI 2009, stable 2012). Key findings: GLU persistently elevated (192, 198 — diabetes not formally treated?), CPK 600 (statin myopathy → ZOCOR switched to ROSUBEN), PRA suppressed 0.18 with ALDo 86 → ALDo/PRA ratio ~478 suggesting primary hyperaldosteronism (Conn), DEXA shows osteopenia/possible osteoporosis (hip T-score discrepancy), Vit D insufficient → treated with BIA.FIX. Visit 8: PTH 209 markedly elevated (prev 48.4) needs verification. Pages 2-4 extremely difficult handwriting with many illegible sections — dense specialist endocrine workup data.

uncertain_rows: 15-16 (ΑΙ, Χ/Γ — unconfirmed abbreviations in father's acoustic neuroma)
B12 value — run-on text, could be 446,20 or 1464,20
SGPT value — appears as 115 but could be 11 or 15
Testosterone 0,90 — v9 correction, still uncertain units/value
Patient004_1_v10.output_format: v10 lab-panel-aware — 3-phase transcription with lab panel templates + original JPG appended
Patient004_1_v10.notes: First v10 transcription. Lab panel templates used to validate all 38 lab values against plausible ranges. 5 template-based corrections (MCHC 334→33,4, Κρεατ 10→1,0, SHBG 78→27,8, Testo 19→0,90, CPK 250→2598). Inherited 6 corrections from v9 fusion (TRG 80→180, γGT 15→46, SGOT added, HbA1c removed, SHBG, Testo). Lab results grouped into 10 panels with panel sub-headings in the document. Key clinical flags: CPK 2598 (>15x ULN), ferritin 303 (elevated premenopausal F), dyslipidemia (CHOL 206, TRG 180, LDL 135), HGB/HCT elevated for female.

uncertain_rows: 15-16 (ΑΙ, Χ/Γ — unconfirmed abbreviations in father's acoustic neuroma)
Patient004_1_v9.output_format: v9 fusion — Google Vision + Claude image reading, fused + medical review
Patient004_1_v9.notes: First v9 fusion transcription. Female, 26yr, psychology student on Zoloft. 7 corrections vs v8: TRG 80→180, CPK 250→2598 (CRITICAL — markedly elevated, consistent with doctor ordering Αλδολάση), SGOT added (15), γGT 15→46, HbA1c removed (hallucination), SHBG 78→27.8, Testosterone 19→0.90 (uncertain). Google Vision was better at: digits in dense lab lines (CPK, TRG, γGT), medication names (Αλδολάση, Rabest). Claude was better at: structure, medical abbreviations (ΟΛΛ, κφ, ΦΤ), section assignment, context. CPK 2598 is the most clinically significant correction — changes entire clinical picture from mild to severe muscle involvement.

uncertain_rows: 15-16 (ΑΙ, Χ/Γ — unconfirmed abbreviations in father's acoustic neuroma)
82-89 (Visit 2 instructions — several items hard to read: ECHO details, Αξονογραφία, RadNet)
Patient004_1_v8.output_format: v8 hybrid — transcription with medical review + original JPG appended
Patient004_1_v8.notes: First v8 transcription. Female, 26yr, psychology student on Zoloft. Presenting: sleep disorder + weight loss. 2 visits. Phase 2 caught all known KB corrections proactively. Key flags: elevated ferritin (303) for premenopausal female, mildly elevated homocysteine (18.0), insufficient vitamin D (24). SUPERSEDED BY V9 — v9 found 7 lab value errors in v8.

Patient004_1_v7.output_format: v7 hybrid — transcription + original JPG appended as full page

uncertain_rows: 33 (Ca Προστάτου under mother — IMPOSSIBLE for female, flagged)
Multiple ECHO rows (57-77) — very dense handwriting, VERY LOW confidence
97-107 (Visit 2 medications and instructions — multiple uncertain names/dosages)
Patient005_1_v8.output_format: v8 hybrid — 3-phase transcription + 2 original JPGs appended
Patient005_1_v8.phase2_time: ~4.6 min (including v7 text extraction for cross-reference)
Patient005_1_v8.notes: Female, 58yr, retired nurse from Serres. Presenting: thyroid function assessment. 2 visits (01/04/2021 + 10/11/2021). Complex case: coronary disease with stenting, endogenous bronchial asthma (15yr), endometriosis, uterine fibroid, chronic constipation. Key findings: subclinical hypothyroidism (TSH 4.77), autoimmune thyroid disease on ECHO, vitamin D deficiency (15.83), osteopenia on DEXA, elevated insulin (56, insulin resistance), very elevated B12 (2328), possible secondary hyperparathyroidism. Phase 2 caught DOB error (31/6→03/06), age error (60→58), address misread, allergy misread (ampicillin→shrimp+red medications). Page 2 ECHO section extremely dense with very low confidence. Weight discrepancy between visits (63→83kg) flagged.

Patient002_1_v3.notes: Fresh Sonnet 4.6 run. 94 rows (vs 108 in v2). Same 3-visit structure.

document_format_v7.description: Hybrid format: structured digital transcription followed by original scan appended at end
document_format_v7.approach: Digitize metadata accurately (patient info, dates, meds, labs), accept imperfect clinical note transcription, append original for reference
document_format_v7.source_format: JPG preferred over PNG (7x smaller for scans, no visible quality loss)
document_format_v7.image_embedding: Original scan goes in a SEPARATE docx section with minimal margins (0.5cm), no headers/footers, image scaled to fill page using transformation width matching page width at 96 DPI
document_format_v7.image_sizing_lesson: docx-js ImageRun transformation uses pixels at 96 DPI. A4 with 0.5cm margins = 200mm × 287mm content area = 756px × 1085px at 96 DPI. Use 750×1070 with ~1% safety buffer. Must use separate section for different margins — cannot mix margin sizes within one section.
document_format_v7.fit_to_page_rule: Always fit-to-page: calculate scaleW = 750/imgW, scaleH = 1070/imgH, use Math.min(scaleW, scaleH). This ensures the ENTIRE image fits without cropping. Wide images are width-constrained, tall images are height-constrained. CRITICAL: images must NEVER be cropped.
document_format_v7.jpg_quality: 75 at 150 DPI is sweet spot for scan embedding (~1.5MB/page). For small source images already at screen res, just embed as-is.

differences_from_others: MOST PAGES (5) and MOST VISITS (8) of any patient — spans 5 years (2014-2019)
No formal clinical exam table found — doctor focused on specialist workup
Multiple imaging modalities: MRI, CT, DEXA, TRIPLEX, ECHO thyroid, ECHO abdomen
Medication changes tracked across visits (ZOCOR → ROSUBEN, FILICINE stopped, BIA.FIX started)
Pages 2-4 extremely dense — specialist notes with lab values crammed into running text
Diagnosis summary box below family tree — unusual format compared to other patients

differences_from_004_005: 3 visits (initial + review results + post-op) — most visits so far
Dense imaging reports: CT thorax, Echo thyroid, Scanning thyroid — all inline as running text
Histopathology report included — macro and microscopic findings
Pre-visit labs/imaging (done before first visit, results brought by patient)
Family tree includes sibling (sister, 46yr) — used fourBoxRow
Pre-op labs (Ca, P, PTH, ECG) documented

differences_from_004: No AMKA recorded for this patient
Family history includes siblings (not just parents)
Multiple diagnostic tests between visits (TRIPLEX, ECHO, DEXA)
Much denser note — 2 pages vs Patient004's single page
Follow-up visit includes additional medication (ΠΡΟΣΘΕΤΗ ΑΓΩΓΗ)
